subject: LU-12323 libcfs: check if save_stack_trace_tsk is exported

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(1 comment)

1 style warning(s).

Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2:

The only place a task_struct is passed in is for ptlrpc service threads. Otherwise its current. The question is does prtlrpc service thread == current when called?

Patch Set 3:

> The only place a task_struct is passed in is for ptlrpc service threads. Otherwise its current. The question is does prtlrpc service thread == current when called?

My understanding is that the ptlrpc watchdog threads are printing the stack traces for _other_ ptlrpc threads (the ones that seem to be stuck).

parents.subject: LU-6142 ldlm: Fix style issues for ldlm_plain.c

commit.subject: LU-12323 libcfs: check if save_stack_trace_tsk is exported
commit.message:
LU-12323 libcfs: check if save_stack_trace_tsk is exported

Lustre 2.12 commit afedf9343686504c89f2e28cf6133540166f2347 introduced
the use of save_stack_trace_tsk, but this symbol is not exported for
all architectures. When it's possible we can use save_stack_trace
instead. Otherwise skip printing stack trace.

Cray-bug-id: LUS-7352
Test-Parameters: clientarch=aarch64
